BaseParameter.description:
A lightweight reference to a controlled vocabulary (CV) term.

Used within constraint definitions to identify specific CV terms
by their label, accession, or human-readable name.

cv_label.description: Short identifier of the controlled vocabulary (e.g. 'MS' for PSI-MS, 'UO' for Unit Ontology).
cv_label.title: Cv Label

cv_accession.description: Accession of the term in CURIE format (e.g. 'MS:1000073').
cv_accession.title: Cv Accession

name.description: Human-readable name of the parameter term (e.g. 'electrospray ionization').
name.title: Name

BaseParameter.title: BaseParameter

BooleanConstraint.description: Validates that a value is boolean.

validator.description: Label of validator. If it is not defined, default validator will be used
validator.title: Validator

json_path.description: Jsonpath used as input to constraint. It is relative jsonpath of `value`. If it is not defined, `value` will be used.
json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: List of values that are exceptions to the constraint. If a value is found in this list, the constraint will not be applied to it and return True.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

exceptional_false_values.description: Exceptional values counted as false. e.g., '0', 'no'.  List all as lower case
exceptional_false_values.title: Exceptional False Values

exceptional_true_values.description: Exceptional true value list. e.g., '1', 'yeah'.  List all as lower case
exceptional_true_values.title: Exceptional True Values

BooleanConstraint.title: BooleanConstraint

CVListConstraint.description:
Restricts CV terms to those from specific controlled vocabularies.

The ``cv_list`` field specifies which CV namespace labels are
allowed (e.g. ``['MS', 'UO']``).

validator.description: Label of validator. If it is not defined, default validator will be used
validator.title: Validator

json_path.description: Jsonpath used as input to constraint. It is relative jsonpath of `value`. If it is not defined, `value` will be used.
json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: Allowed exceptional CV terms.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

allow_user_defined_terms.description: Whether user-defined terms (empty cv_label and cv_accession) are accepted alongside CV terms.
allow_user_defined_terms.title: Allow User Defined Terms

is_cv_term_value_required.description: Whether the CV term's value slot must be populated. When True, value MUST not be empty or null.
is_cv_term_value_required.title: Is Cv Term Value Required

allowed_cv_list.description: List of allowed CV namespace labels (e.g. ['MS', 'UO', 'PRIDE']). Use only uppercase letters.None means any CV namespace is accepted.
allowed_cv_list.title: Allowed Cv List

CVListConstraint.title: CVListConstraint

CVTermConstraint.description:
Base constraint for values that must be controlled vocabulary terms.

Subclasses refine which CV terms are acceptable (by list, by parent
term, or by CV namespace). This class captures the common options
shared across all CV term constraints.

validator.description: Label of validator. If it is not defined, default validator will be used
validator.title: Validator

json_path.description: Jsonpath used as input to constraint. It is relative jsonpath of `value`. If it is not defined, `value` will be used.
json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: Allowed exceptional CV terms.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

allow_user_defined_terms.description: Whether user-defined terms (empty cv_label and cv_accession) are accepted alongside CV terms.
allow_user_defined_terms.title: Allow User Defined Terms

is_cv_term_value_required.description: Whether the CV term's value slot must be populated. When True, value MUST not be empty or null.
is_cv_term_value_required.title: Is Cv Term Value Required

CVTermConstraint.title: CVTermConstraint

CVTermEnumConstraint.description:
Restricts the value to a specific set of CV terms.

Unlike ``CVListConstraint`` which filters by CV namespace, this
constraint enumerates the exact terms that are acceptable.

validator.description: Label of validator. If it is not defined, default validator will be used
validator.title: Validator

json_path.description: Jsonpath used as input to constraint. It is relative jsonpath of `value`. If it is not defined, `value` will be used.
json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: Allowed exceptional CV terms.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

allow_user_defined_terms.description: Whether user-defined terms (empty cv_label and cv_accession) are accepted alongside CV terms.
allow_user_defined_terms.title: Allow User Defined Terms

is_cv_term_value_required.description: Whether the CV term's value slot must be populated. When True, value MUST not be empty or null.
is_cv_term_value_required.title: Is Cv Term Value Required

allowed_cv_terms.description: Explicit list of allowed CV terms. Each entry identifies a term by label, accession, or name.
allowed_cv_terms.title: Allowed Cv Terms

CVTermEnumConstraint.title: CVTermEnumConstraint

DateTimeConstraint.description:
Validates that a string value represents a valid date, time,
or datetime.

When ``format`` is provided, the value must match that specific
pattern (e.g. ISO-8601). When omitted, ISO-8601 is accepted.

validator.description: Label of validator. If it is not defined, default validator will be used
validator.title: Validator

json_path.description: Jsonpath used as input to constraint. It is relative jsonpath of `value`. If it is not defined, `value` will be used.
json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: List of values that are exceptions to the constraint. If a value is found in this list, the constraint will not be applied to it and return True.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

format.description: Expected time/date format string (e.g. '%Y-%m-%d', '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', '%H:%M:%S'). None means Date and time in UTC format is accepted.
format.title: Format

DateTimeConstraint.title: DateTimeConstraint

DecimalConstraint.description:
Validates that a value is a valid floating-point number.

Supports optional range bounds, scientific notation control,
and decimal precision limits.

validator.description: Label of validator. If it is not defined, default validator will be used
validator.title: Validator

json_path.description: Jsonpath used as input to constraint. It is relative jsonpath of `value`. If it is not defined, `value` will be used.
json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: List of values that are exceptions to the constraint. If a value is found in this list, the constraint will not be applied to it and return True.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

minimum.description: Minimum allowed value (inclusive). None means no lower bound.
minimum.title: Minimum

maximum.description: Maximum allowed value (inclusive). None means no upper bound.
maximum.title: Maximum

allow_scientific_notation.description: Whether scientific notation (e.g. '1.23e4') is accepted. None means no restriction.
allow_scientific_notation.title: Allow Scientific Notation

min_scale.description: Minimum number of digits after the decimal point. None means no minimum scale requirement.
min_scale.title: Min Scale

max_scale.description: Maximum number of digits after the decimal point. None means no maximum scale limit.
max_scale.title: Max Scale

DecimalConstraint.title: DecimalConstraint

root_value_evaluation.title: Root Value Evaluation

default_evaluation.title: Default Evaluation

min_valid.description: Minimum number of constraints that must be true for the group to be true.
min_valid.title: Min Valid

max_valid.description: Maximum number of constraints that can be true for the group to be true.
max_valid.title: Max Valid

name.title: Name

value.description: The user value for the parameter.
value.title: Value

code.description: Requirement id. It is highly recommended to use unique code for each requirement
code.title: Code

IntegerConstraint.description:
Validates that a value is a valid integer within an optional range.

Use ``PositiveIntegerConstraint`` or ``NonNegativeIntegerConstraint``
for common presets.

default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: List of values that are exceptions to the constraint. If a value is found in this list, the constraint will not be applied to it and return True.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

minimum.description: Minimum allowed value (inclusive). None means no lower bound.
minimum.title: Minimum

maximum.description: Maximum allowed value (inclusive). None means no upper bound.
maximum.title: Maximum

IntegerConstraint.title: IntegerConstraint

IntegerEnumConstraint.description:
Validates that an integer value is one of a fixed set of allowed options.

Each option maps an integer key to a display value, integer code,
or an ``ExtendedParameter`` with full CV term metadata.

validator.title: Validator

negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: List of values that are exceptions to the constraint. If a value is found in this list, the constraint will not be applied to it and return True.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

options.description: Mapping of allowed integer values to their display labels, integer codes, or CV term definitions.
options.title: Options

IntegerEnumConstraint.title: IntegerEnumConstraint

profile_validator_factory_class_argument.title: Profile Validator Factory Class Arguments

ParentCVTermConstraint.description:
Restricts the value to CV terms that are descendants of
specified parent terms in the ontology hierarchy.

Supports exclusion of specific terms or name patterns to
filter out unwanted branches of the ontology tree.

validator.description: Label of validator. If it is not defined, default validator will be used
validator.title: Validator

json_path.description: Jsonpath used as input to constraint. It is relative jsonpath of `value`. If it is not defined, `value` will be used.
json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: Allowed exceptional CV terms.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

allow_user_defined_terms.description: Whether user-defined terms (empty cv_label and cv_accession) are accepted alongside CV terms.
allow_user_defined_terms.title: Allow User Defined Terms

is_cv_term_value_required.description: Whether the CV term's value slot must be populated. When True, value MUST not be empty or null.
is_cv_term_value_required.title: Is Cv Term Value Required

parent_cv_terms.description: List of parent CV terms. Valid values must be descendants of at least one of these terms.
parent_cv_terms.title: Parent Cv Terms

excluded_cv_terms.description: CV terms to exclude even if they are descendants of a parent term.
excluded_cv_terms.title: Excluded Cv Terms

ParentCVTermConstraint.title: ParentCVTermConstraint

RegexConstraint.description:
Validates a string value against a regular expression pattern.

Use for structured identifiers or format patterns that can be
expressed as a single regex (e.g. ORCID, adduct ion notation).

validator.description: Label of validator. If it is not defined, default validator will be used
validator.title: Validator

json_path.description: Jsonpath used as input to constraint. It is relative jsonpath of `value`. If it is not defined, `value` will be used.
json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: List of values that are exceptions to the constraint. If a value is found in this list, the constraint will not be applied to it and return True.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern the value must match. Anchored implicitly (full-match semantics).
pattern.title: Pattern

case_sensitive.description: Whether the pattern match is case-sensitive. Defaults to True.
case_sensitive.title: Case Sensitive

RegexConstraint.title: RegexConstraint

StringConstraint.description:
Validates that a string value's length falls within a range.

At least one of ``minimum`` or ``maximum`` should be set.

validator.title: Validator

json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: List of values that are exceptions to the constraint. If a value is found in this list, the constraint will not be applied to it and return True.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

minimum.description: Minimum number of characters (inclusive). None means no lower bound.
minimum.title: Minimum

maximum.description: Maximum number of characters (inclusive). None means no upper bound.
maximum.title: Maximum

StringConstraint.title: StringConstraint

Validates that a string value is one of a fixed set of allowed options.

Each option maps a string key to a display value, integer code,
or an ``ExtendedParameter`` with full CV term metadata.

validator.title: Validator

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: List of values that are exceptions to the constraint. If a value is found in this list, the constraint will not be applied to it and return True.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

options.description: Mapping of allowed string values to their display labels, integer codes, or CV term definitions.
options.title: Options

StringEnumConstraint.title: StringEnumConstraint

UriConstraint.description:
Validates that a string value is a well-formed URI.

Optionally restricts accepted URI schemes (e.g. only ``https``,
``ftp``).

validator.description: Label of validator. If it is not defined, default validator will be used
validator.title: Validator

json_path.description: Jsonpath used as input to constraint. It is relative jsonpath of `value`. If it is not defined, `value` will be used.
json_path.title: Json Path

default_precondition_evaluation.description: Defines evaluation value if there is a precondition failure. if it is not defined, evaluation value will be considered True
default_precondition_evaluation.title: Default Precondition Evaluation

negated.description: Whether the constraint should be negated. If set to true, the constraint is inverted.
negated.title: Negated

null_values.description: List of values that should be considered null.
null_values.title: Null Values

exceptional_values.description: List of values that are exceptions to the constraint. If a value is found in this list, the constraint will not be applied to it and return True.
exceptional_values.title: Exceptional Values

allowed_schemes.description: List of accepted URI schemes (e.g. ['http', 'https', 'ftp']). None means any scheme is accepted.
allowed_schemes.title: Allowed Schemes

UriConstraint.title: UriConstraint
